extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with under 3km to go after VINGEGAARD launched earlier, took the stage but VINGEGAARD gained yellow via intermediate bonuses accumulated through the stage
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma attacked from kilometer zero with 80km remaining, using HOFSTETTER in break to soften Bora-controlled pace before finale.
POGAČAR waited for VINGEGAARD's attack with 3km to go then countered immediately, reversing momentum despite appearing isolated on Tourmalet.
VINGEGAARD took yellow despite losing stage by 20+ seconds through accumulated bonus seconds strategy, not just summit finish time.

rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: negative: lost yellow jersey and dropped 4km from Tourmalet summit, suggesting week 1 leadership was unsustainable
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: negative: dropped early on Tourmalet when pace intensified, major podium threat eliminated before decisive kilometers
rider_signals.JOHANNESSEN Tobias: positive: finished third behind two Grand Tour champions, only other rider to follow late accelerations
rider_signals.RODRIGUEZ Carlos: neutral: best Ineos rider but finished with Hindley group, unable to match top two's accelerations
